title: Marinated Quail with Dill Relish
description: This high-protein-friendly Venezuelan dish features a savory marination and a tangy dill relish that complements the crispy quail meat beautifully.

ingredients.name: vinegar
ingredients.notes: like apple cider or rice vinegar

instructions: Prepare a spice blend: In a small bowl, mix together salt and black pepper. Set aside.
In a large shallow dish or zip-top bag, combine the spice blend, vinegar, lemon juice, garlic, dill, olive oil, and any leftover spices from the spice blend.

Place quail breasts in the marinade and ensure they are fully coated. Cover tightly with plastic wrap or place in an airtight container. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight for best flavor development.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Remove quail from marinade, discarding any remaining liquid. Discard spices used for the marination if you prefer not to re-use them.

Place coated quails on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a silicone mat. Bake in preheated oven for about 20 minutes or until cooked through (internal temperature reaches at least 165°F/74°C).
Meanwhile, prepare the dill relish: In a small bowl, mix together the remaining fresh or dried dill with the minced garlic and lemon juice. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Garnish each quail breast with a spoonful of dill relish before serving to enhance its flavor profile.
